 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn","title":"Fix results PageUp/PageDown to page selection deterministically","description":"## Goal
Make results-surface `PageUp`/`PageDown` navigation deterministic and user-visible by advancing selection page-wise while keeping viewport/scroll state coherent.

## Background
During post-close revalidation of the FrankenTUI results regression suite, we confirmed a semantics mismatch:
- `CassMsg::PageScrolled` currently manipulates virtualized scroll state, then synchronizes selection from stale state.
- In practice this can behave like a weak/no-op page action depending on viewport initialization and subsequent selection syncing.

Users expect page keys to provide clear navigation progress (selection + viewport movement), especially in long result lists.

## Scope
- Update results-focus handling for `CassMsg::PageScrolled` in `src/ui/app.rs` so page navigation:
  - advances/retreats selection by one viewport page (with clamped bounds),
  - keeps virtualized list offset consistent with selected row visibility,
  - remains deterministic before and after render cycles.
- Preserve existing detail-pane page scroll behavior (`FocusRegion::Detail`) unchanged.
- Keep `Home/End` semantics unchanged.

## Test Plan
Add/adjust unit tests in `src/ui/app.rs` covering:
1. PageDown from top advances selected row by at least one page when enough rows exist.
2. PageUp from a paged position retreats selected row and never underflows.
3. Repeated paging reaches terminal bounds deterministically.
4. Selection remains visible after render (no snap-back regressions).
5. Detail-pane page scroll path still adjusts `detail_scroll` and does not affect result selection.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `PageUp`/`PageDown` provide obvious selection movement in long result sets.
- No regression in detail-pane scroll behavior.
- Existing results navigation tests continue to pass.
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` all pass.

## Risks / Tradeoffs
- Changing page-key semantics may affect any tests or implicit assumptions that treated page keys as offset-only behavior.
- Mitigation: keep behavior explicit, covered by tests, and scoped to results-focus path only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:27:52.183684749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:31:06.990519910Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:31:06.990491677Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic PageUp/PageDown selection paging + regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","navigation","results"],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution evidence for `coding_agent_session_search-1l0pn` (2026-02-09)

Scope delivered
- Updated results `PageUp/PageDown` handling to move selection by viewport-sized steps instead of relying on offset-only page scrolling.
- Preserved detail-pane page scrolling behavior unchanged.
- Added deterministic tests for paging movement, bounds clamping, and detail-path isolation.

Touched surfaces
- Runtime behavior: `CassMsg::PageScrolled` in `src/ui/app.rs:9667`
  - Results focus path now computes `page_size = visible_count.max(1)`.
  - Selection advances/retreats by page-sized step with bound clamps.
  - Virtualized state is synced via `state.select(Some(target))`.
  - Detail path still uses `detail_scroll += delta * 20`.

- Regression tests: `src/ui/app.rs`
  - `results_navigation_page_scroll_and_home_end_are_stable` (`src/ui/app.rs:16360`)
  - `repeated_page_scroll_clamps_at_bounds` (`src/ui/app.rs:16400`)
  - `detail_page_scroll_does_not_mutate_result_selection` (`src/ui/app.rs:16425`)

Deterministic validation
- `cargo test --lib results_navigation_page_scroll_and_home_end_are_stable -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib repeated_page_scroll_clamps_at_bounds -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib detail_page_scroll_does_not_mutate_result_selection -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib results_surface_density_theme_matrix_preserves_core_cues -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib density_mode_switch_preserves_selection_and_scroll_state -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test --lib results_focus_persists_through_theme_and_filter_changes -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

Tradeoffs
- Paging now intentionally prioritizes visible selection movement (orientation) over previous offset-centric semantics.
- Step size is driven by last rendered viewport visibility; fallback keeps progress deterministic when visibility is not initialized.

Residual risks / follow-up
- If product direction wants scroll-anchor-preserving paging (keeping same relative row line), a follow-up can refine offset strategy while keeping current selection guarantees.
","created_at":"2026-02-09T05:31:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lcw","title":"T3.1: Audit Rust tests for E2eLogger usage","description":"Audit all Rust integration tests and add E2eLogger where missing.

## Scope
- Review all tests/*.rs files
- Identify tests not using E2eLogger
- Add structured logging to each test

## Key Tests to Audit
- pages_master_e2e.rs (already uses logger)
- e2e_cli_flows.rs
- e2e_filters.rs
- connector_*.rs tests
- search_pipeline.rs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All E2E tests emit JSONL logs
- [ ] Test names and suites correctly tagged
- [ ] File and line info included
- [ ] Duration captured for all tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:22:13.259657737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:21:58.293015475Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:21:58.292941518Z","close_reason":"Audit complete: Added E2eLogger infrastructure to e2e_cli_flows.rs as reference implementation. Found 111+ tests across 8 E2E files needing conversion. Created run_logged_test helper for incremental adoption.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lcw","depends_on_id":"30qc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lumv","title":"Fix progress percentage u8 overflow in model_download.rs","description":"When total_downloaded exceeds grand_total due to filesystem race conditions during model downloads, the percentage calculation could overflow u8 range. Fixed by clamping with .min(100.0) before casting to u8.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-21T22:26:21.373137774Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T22:26:32.082456154Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T22:26:32.081830793Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lwxf","title":"Phase 4K: Clipboard integration via OSC52","description":"Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration that works over SSH and in any modern terminal. Add clipboard operations: (1) Copy selected result path+line to clipboard (Ctrl+Y or y), (2) Copy detail pane content to clipboard (Ctrl+C when in detail view without selection), (3) Copy code block from detail view (click or keybinding on specific code block), (4) Copy search query to clipboard, (5) Paste from clipboard into search bar (Ctrl+V -- currently handled by crossterm Paste event, but ftui clipboard provides a unified API). OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without any external dependency -- it works over SSH sessions which is critical for cass multi-machine search. The clipboard module handles terminal capability detection and graceful fallback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:21:52.504734512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027618302Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:33.027591773Z","close_reason":"Content preserved in new 2noh9.3 bead: Clipboard OSC52 integration. Copy paths, content, code blocks; works over SSH merged.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1lwxf","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1p0wb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mag","title":"Replace e2e_install_easy fake binaries with real install flow","description":"Eliminate fake rustc/cargo/sha256sum stubs in tests/e2e_install_easy.rs.\n\nDetails:\n- Run install.sh against a real rustup toolchain in a temp HOME (CI + local opt-in).\n- Capture full install logs and verify binary checksums.\n- Keep a fast-path smoke test if needed, but remove fake-bin allowlist entry.","acceptance_criteria":"1) e2e_install_easy runs install.sh with real rustup/cargo in temp HOME.
2) Logs capture stdout/stderr and checksum verification.
3) Fake-bin allowlist entry removed (unless documented exception).
4) Test can be gated by env for local runs; always runs in CI.","notes":"Notes:
- Prefer a dedicated CI job for real install flow.
- Ensure cleanup does not delete user data.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T18:13:23.114573078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:06.963139681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:43:06.963000452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mag","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-ul61","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","title":"FrankenTUI UX revelation pass for cass TUI","description":"FrankenTUI UX Revelation Program for cass.

Why this exists:
The current cass TUI has strong fundamentals (virtualized rendering, adaptive degradation, robust tests), but still under-leverages FrankenTUI's differentiators: explainable runtime decisions, evidence-driven interaction UX, and regime-aware responsiveness under input/resize stress.

Strategic intent:
1. Make the UI feel materially more responsive under real-world terminal conditions.
2. Make adaptive behavior debuggable and explainable in-product.
3. Raise interaction quality so command, resize, and pointer behavior are stable and predictable.
4. Keep implementation decisions auditable via tests and explicit acceptance criteria.

Program structure:
- .1 Command palette migration to evidence-driven FrankenTUI patterns.
- .2 BOCPD resize coalescer + decision evidence surfaced in cass UI.
- .3 Explainability cockpit overlay/paneling (diff/resize/budget/timeline).
- .4 Structural responsive layouts by breakpoint topology.
- .5 Input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization) [already completed].
- .6 Program-level hardening + release gates.

Dependency policy:
- Runtime evidence plumbing (.2) must precede full cockpit timeline capabilities (.3).
- Palette migration (.1) and responsive layouts (.4) can progress in parallel.
- Hardening/release gate (.6) blocks final close of this epic.

Success definition:
- Core workstreams .1 .2 .3 .4 implemented with green quality gates.
- .6 hardening confirms no regressions in stability/perf/usability.
- New behavior is self-explanatory through inspector/cockpit signals.
- Beads are sufficient for future contributors without external planning docs.","design":"Architecture principle: observable adaptivity.

Every adaptive decision (diff/resize/budget/interaction) should be represented as:
1) deterministic runtime behavior,
2) evidence that can be surfaced in UI,
3) tests that lock in expected behavior.

This avoids black-box UX drift and enables root-cause debugging directly from cass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:24.570821358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T04:05:39.895097746Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T04:05:39.895072659Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":150,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program note: prioritize functional responsiveness and explainability over visual novelty. If a proposed UI change looks flashy but does not improve operator speed, stability, or debuggability, it is out of scope for this epic. The highest leverage is in making adaptive behavior legible and trustworthy.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:33Z"}]}
